SEARCH
Package client.view

Class ReservationListController

java.lang.Object
client.view.BaseController
client.view.ReservationListController
public class ReservationListController extends BaseController
"My Reservations" screen: look up a visitor's reservations by id and manage them. Each row offers Confirm / Cancel, enabled only for statuses where the action is legal. The disabling is a UX hint — the server independently enforces the legal transitions, so a stale or tampered client cannot drive an illegal one.

Realtime push: after each successful load the screen subscribes (through the BaseController.subscribe(java.lang.String, long, java.util.function.Consumer<common.dto.ServerEvent>) helper) to every reservation id on screen, so a confirm/cancel/update committed by another client refreshes the list within ~1s. The push handler only re-reads the list (never a mutating op), so applying a pushed change cannot loop back into another publish. BaseController.onHide() auto-unsubscribes when the screen is navigated away.

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      private void initialize()
      Initializes the controller after its FXML fields are injected.

    • parkNamesFrom

      private Map<Integer,String> parkNamesFrom(ServerResponse res)
      Builds the park id -> name lookup from a LIST_PARKS response (defensive, never throws); mirrors the Dashboard's parkNamesFrom.

    • parkName

      private String parkName(int parkId)
      The park's name, or a "Park #" fallback when the names aren't loaded.

    • onLoad

      private void onLoad()
      Performs the on load operation.

    • loadFor

      private void loadFor(long visitorId)
      Performs the load for operation.
      Parameters:
      visitorId - value supplied to the operation

    • populate

      private void populate(List<ReservationDTO> rows)
      Performs the populate operation.
      Parameters:
      rows - value supplied to the operation

    • resubscribe

      private void resubscribe(List<ReservationDTO> rows)
      Points this screen's realtime subscriptions at exactly the reservation ids currently displayed. Drops the previous set first via the BaseController.unsubscribeAll() helper (which also sends the matching UNSUBSCRIBE), so a fresh Load or a push-driven refresh never leaks subscriptions; BaseController.onHide() drops the rest on navigation.

    • onReservationEvent

      private void onReservationEvent(ServerEvent ev)
      Reacts to a pushed reservation change. Runs on the FX thread (the EventBus marshals via Platform.runLater). It only re-reads the visitor's list (LIST_RESERVATIONS is read-only and publishes nothing), so applying a pushed change can never re-trigger a publish and loop.
      Parameters:
      ev - the pushed event (its id is one of the displayed reservations)

    • headerRow

      private javafx.scene.layout.HBox headerRow()
      Performs the header row operation.
      Returns:
      the result produced by the operation

    • flexSpacer

      private javafx.scene.layout.Region flexSpacer()
      A zero-width filler that absorbs the row's slack so fixed columns and the action buttons keep their natural widths instead of being squeezed.

    • dataRow

      private javafx.scene.layout.HBox dataRow(ReservationDTO r, boolean withDivider)
      Performs the data row operation.
      Parameters:
      r - value supplied to the operation
      withDivider - value supplied to the operation
      Returns:
      the result produced by the operation

    • clickHitButton

      private boolean clickHitButton(Object target)
      True if the click landed on a Button (or a node inside one), so the row's click handler should defer to that button's own action instead of opening the detail view.

    • showDetail

      private void showDetail(ReservationDTO r)
      Opens a small read-only detail of one reservation: every field the row hints at, spelled out — date, time, party, type, status, price, payment, guide and confirmation code. View-only by design: a single Close button and no editable controls (rescheduling stays on the Edit action). All values come from the ReservationDTO already loaded, so opening a detail never calls the server.
      Parameters:
      r - the reservation whose row was clicked

    • addDetail

      private void addDetail(javafx.scene.layout.GridPane grid, int row, String key, String value)
      Adds one read-only "key: value" line to the detail grid.

    • orDash

      private String orDash(String value)
      Renders a blank/null field as an em dash so empty values read cleanly.

    • canVisitorExit

      private boolean canVisitorExit(ReservationDTO r)
      Visitor self-exit is offered only to the logged-in owner of a confirmed booking.

    • visitorExit

      private void visitorExit(int reservationId)
      Lets the ordering visitor mark the whole reservation group as exited. The server verifies ownership and that the party actually has an open visit.

    • canCancel

      private boolean canCancel(ReservationStatus s)
      Cancel is legal from PENDING, CONFIRMED or WAITING (mirrors the server rule).

    • canEdit

      private boolean canEdit(ReservationStatus s)
      Edit (reschedule) is legal only from PENDING or CONFIRMED (mirrors the server rule).

    • confirm

      private void confirm(int reservationId)
      Performs the confirm operation.
      Parameters:
      reservationId - value supplied to the operation

    • cancel

      private void cancel(int reservationId)
      Performs the cancel operation.
      Parameters:
      reservationId - value supplied to the operation

    • confirmAction

      private boolean confirmAction(String header, String content)
      Shows a blocking Yes/No confirmation and returns true only if the user clicked Yes. Both Confirm and Cancel are guarded by this so a stray click never fires the op without the user agreeing first. Yes/No (rather than OK/Cancel) avoids a confusing "Cancel" button on the cancel-reservation prompt.
      Parameters:
      header - the bold dialog header (the question)
      content - the explanatory body line
      Returns:
      whether the user confirmed

    • edit

      private void edit(ReservationDTO r)
      Launches the on-screen edit flow for one reservation, replacing the old popup dialog: a three-step wizard — Edit Details → Review → Settlement — with a step indicator, rendered in place of the list. Behaviour is unchanged from the dialog: a date / optional-time / party edit, then UPDATE_RESERVATION (the server re-checks status, group cap and capacity and recomputes the price), the collect/refund/quote settlement, and a live list refresh — only the presentation is richer.

      User-initiated only. A pushed update never lands here — the push handler (onReservationEvent(common.dto.ServerEvent)) only re-reads the list — so applying an edit cannot loop back into another update.

    • settlementMessage

      private String settlementMessage(ReservationUpdateResultDTO result)
      Builds the (simulated) settlement line for a price change after an edit. No real gateway is involved — it only tells the operator what to do: prepaid + price up → collect the difference; prepaid + price down → refund; pay-on- arrival → quote the new total due at the gate. Returns null when the price didn't change (e.g. only the date moved) so the caller can say so.
      Parameters:
      result - the server's update result carrying the old and new price
      Returns:
      the settlement message, or null for a zero delta

    • buildStepIndicator

      private javafx.scene.layout.HBox buildStepIndicator(int active, String[] labels)
      Builds the step indicator for the edit wizard, mirroring the old Update-Order wizard: a row of numbered circles + captions joined by connector lines, with completed steps ticked and the active one highlighted. Styled by the step-* classes in client.css.
      Parameters:
      active - the zero-based index of the current step
      labels - the per-step captions (their count drives the number of dots)

    • reload

      private void reload()
      Performs the reload operation.

      w - value supplied to the operation
      Returns:
      the result produced by the operation
